prompt:
Passage 1
A regional maintenance department recently changed how it handles requests for replacement filters. Previously, employees sent requests by email to a shared inbox. During busy periods, messages could be overlooked or duplicated, causing some filters to be ordered twice while others were not ordered at all.
The department introduced a simple online request form. Employees now select the filter type, equipment location, quantity needed, and urgency level. The form sends the information to the maintenance planner, who reviews requests each afternoon and combines orders when possible.
Managers report that the new process has reduced duplicate orders and made it easier to track which equipment needs replacement filters. Employees also say the form is faster than writing a full email.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The department stopped replacing filters.
choices.B: An online request form improved the filter-ordering process.
choices.C: Employees now order all equipment without approval.
choices.D: Email requests were never used by the department.

explanation: The passage focuses on how the online request form reduced duplicate orders and made filter requests easier to track.

prompt:
Passage 2
NOTICE
Beginning August 12, all employees assigned to the loading area must use the updated pallet inspection checklist before moving loaded pallets. The checklist includes pallet condition, load stability, wrapping, and label visibility.
The updated checklist was created after supervisors noticed that some damaged pallets were being moved before problems were identified. The goal is to help employees catch issues before pallets are transported across the facility.
Completed checklists should be submitted to the shift lead at the end of each shift.

What is the purpose of the notice?

choices.A: To announce an updated pallet inspection checklist
choices.B: To cancel work in the loading area
choices.C: To explain a new employee parking plan

explanation: The notice announces the updated pallet inspection checklist for employees in the loading area.

prompt:
Passage 3
Many workplaces use maintenance histories to understand how equipment has performed over time. A maintenance history may include repair dates, parts replaced, inspection results, technician notes, and the amount of time equipment was out of service.
These records help managers make better decisions. If a machine requires frequent repairs, the company may decide to schedule additional inspections, replace a worn component, or evaluate whether the equipment should eventually be replaced. Without maintenance histories, each repair may seem like an isolated event.
Maintenance histories are most useful when technicians enter clear and complete notes. A note that simply says “fixed problem” may not help the next technician understand what was wrong or how it was corrected.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Maintenance histories help organizations understand and manage equipment problems.
choices.B: Equipment repairs should never be documented.
choices.C: Technician notes are useful only when they are vague.
choices.D: Machines should be replaced after every repair.

explanation: The passage explains that maintenance histories help managers understand equipment problems and make better repair or replacement decisions.

prompt:
Passage 4
Before reliable package tracking became common, customers often had limited information after an item was shipped. A company might confirm that a package had left the warehouse, but the customer would not know where it was during transit or when it might arrive.
Modern package tracking systems changed this experience. Carriers scan packages at different points, such as warehouses, sorting centers, and delivery vehicles. These scans create updates that customers and businesses can view online.
Tracking does not prevent every delay, but it improves visibility. Customers can plan for deliveries more easily, and businesses can respond more quickly when a shipment appears to be delayed or misrouted.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Package tracking makes all deliveries arrive early.
choices.B: Package tracking improved visibility for customers and businesses.
choices.C: Customers should avoid checking delivery updates.

explanation: The passage explains that package tracking gives customers and businesses better information about shipments.

prompt:
Passage 5
A technical training program recently added a short workplace reading section to its weekly lessons. Instructors noticed that some students could understand spoken instructions but had difficulty using written directions, especially when directions included several steps.
The new section asks students to read short workplace-style texts such as notices, equipment instructions, and training reminders. Students practice identifying the main action required, the deadline, and any conditions that must be met before work begins.
Instructors believe this practice will help students become more comfortable with written information they may encounter in apprenticeship training or on the job. Early feedback suggests that students appreciate practicing with realistic materials rather than long academic passages.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The program removed all reading instruction.
choices.B: Workplace-style reading practice was added to help students use written directions.
choices.C: Students now study only long academic passages.
choices.D: Spoken instructions are no longer used in training.

explanation: The passage focuses on adding workplace-style reading practice to help students use written directions.

prompt:
Passage 6
Many companies use near-miss reporting to improve workplace safety. A near miss is an event that could have caused injury or damage but did not. For example, a tool falling from a shelf without hitting anyone may be considered a near miss.
Reporting near misses helps organizations identify hazards before someone is hurt. If several reports involve items falling from the same shelf area, supervisors may check storage practices, shelf condition, or employee access routes.
Near-miss reporting works best when employees understand that the goal is prevention, not blame. Workers are more likely to report problems when they believe the information will be used to improve safety.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Near-miss reporting helps workplaces prevent safety problems before injuries occur.
choices.B: Near misses should be ignored if no one is hurt.
choices.C: Falling tools are the only type of workplace hazard.
choices.D: Safety reports are used only to punish employees.

explanation: The passage explains that reporting near misses can help workplaces identify hazards before injuries occur.

prompt:
Passage 7
Many communities use water-use restrictions during periods of drought. These restrictions may limit lawn watering, car washing, or the use of decorative fountains. The exact rules often depend on water supply levels and local conditions.
The purpose of restrictions is to reduce unnecessary water use while preserving enough water for essential needs such as drinking, sanitation, firefighting, and basic business operations. Public cooperation is important because small reductions by many users can produce meaningful savings.
Communities may adjust restrictions as conditions change. If rainfall improves water supplies, rules may be relaxed. If drought becomes more severe, additional limits may be added.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.A: How communities use restrictions to manage water during drought
choices.B: Why communities should stop monitoring water supplies
choices.C: How decorative fountains are manufactured
choices.D: Why rainfall has no effect on water rules

explanation: The passage explains how water-use restrictions help communities manage limited water supplies during drought.

prompt:
Passage 8
A manufacturing company recently reviewed its training for new machine operators. The review found that employees usually understood classroom explanations but sometimes felt uncertain when applying the information on the production floor.
To address this issue, the company added guided practice sessions after classroom instruction. During these sessions, new operators work beside experienced employees, practice normal startup steps, and ask questions before operating independently.
Supervisors report that the added practice has helped new operators gain confidence and make fewer early mistakes. The company plans to continue reviewing training results to determine whether additional support is needed.

Why did the company review its training?

choices.A: New operators sometimes felt uncertain applying classroom information on the production floor.
choices.B: Classroom instruction was never used.
choices.C: Experienced employees refused to answer questions.
choices.D: Machines were removed from the production floor.

explanation: The company reviewed training because new operators sometimes felt unsure when applying classroom information on the production floor.
